issues.summary: BcqlHandlers retains 'limit' as a deprecated backward-compat alias for 'top' with no documented removal timeline or external consumer inventory
issues.suggestion: Audit all callers of /api/bcql (webapp JS, test suites, curl examples) to verify whether 'limit' is used. If no callers use 'limit', remove the alias in the next release. If callers exist, document them explicitly in the comment and set a removal milestone.
evidence: The Javadoc says 'The deprecated limit field is also accepted for backward compatibility.' No removal target, no issue tracker reference, and no note on whether any client actually uses 'limit'.
If the API is internal (the webapp is served alongside the API), 'limit' usage can be audited by grepping webapp/ or scanning network logs — the compat shim may already be safe to remove.

issues.summary: RelationConfig supports 'tag=' as a legacy grammar attribute alongside current 'xpos=', without a documented migration plan or known active legacy grammars
issues.suggestion: Grep grammar JSON files for 'tag=' attributes. If none exist, remove the posGroupForPrefix fallback call and the 'tag=' branch entirely. If legacy grammars exist, name them explicitly in the comment and set a migration deadline.
evidence: posGroupForPrefix is called twice: once with 'xpos=' (primary) and once with 'tag=' (fallback). The comment states 'Kept to remain compatible with older grammar configs not yet migrated to xpos='.
No test or example uses 'tag=' in the grammar. If all active grammar files use 'xpos=', the fallback is dead code.

issues.summary: Stale .class files (PatternSubstitution.class, HttpApiUtils.class, WordSketchApiServer*.class) are committed to the source tree alongside .java files
issues.suggestion: 1. Add '*.class' to .gitignore (or add /src/**/*.class exclusion) to prevent future commits. 2. Remove all .class files from the repository: git rm src/main/java/pl/marcinmilkowski/word_sketch/api/*.class. 3. Commit with message 'Remove stale compiled artifacts from source tree'. Verify mvn clean package still produces correct output afterward.
evidence: ls output shows .class files in api/ directory alongside .java source files: HttpApiUtils.class (2615 bytes), PatternSubstitution.class (2891 bytes), WordSketchApiServer.class (31889 bytes).
PatternSubstitution.class exists with no corresponding .java source — this is a compiled artifact from a deleted class that was never cleaned from version control.
Committed .class files mislead IDE indexers, confuse directory listings, and indicate the repository lacks a .gitignore entry for compiled output.

issues.summary: RelationUtils lives in config/ but is primarily consumed by api/ for HTTP request alias resolution, creating an import-affinity mismatch
issues.suggestion: Move RelationUtils to the api package where it is consumed. Alternatively, if alias resolution could be needed by non-HTTP callers in future, rename it to RelationAliasResolver and add a brief comment explaining its HTTP-boundary role. Either way, document the intended caller contract.
evidence: RelationUtils contains a RELATION_ALIASES map (8 entries) and resolveRelationAlias() — its sole purpose is mapping HTTP query parameter aliases to grammar config IDs.
It is imported by ExplorationHandlers in the api package. No other package imports it.
The logic serves an HTTP-boundary concern (translating user-facing short names to internal IDs) rather than a config-loading concern.

issues.summary: QueryExecutor.executeDependencyPatternWithPos documents headPosConstraint as non-null but the implementation does not enforce this, allowing null to silently produce a malformed BCQL pattern
issues.suggestion: Add Objects.requireNonNull(headPosConstraint, "headPosConstraint must not be null") at the start of BlackLabQueryExecutor.executeDependencyPatternWithPos. Also add @Nullable or @NonNull (jspecify) to the parameter declaration in both the interface and implementation.
evidence: QueryExecutor.executeDependencyPatternWithPos Javadoc: '@param headPosConstraint POS regex for the head token (e.g., VB.*); must not be null'.
BlackLabQueryExecutor.executeDependencyPatternWithPos only checks 'if (lemma == null || lemma.isEmpty() || deprel == null)' — headPosConstraint is not checked.
String.format with a null headPosConstraint would produce '[lemma="x" & xpos="null"]', which is a valid BCQL string but an incorrect query — the failure is silent and deferred to query execution.

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor.findCollocations silently returns empty list for null/empty lemma, but this behavior is not mentioned in the QueryExecutor interface contract
issues.suggestion: Add to QueryExecutor.findCollocations Javadoc: '@param lemma the head lemma; passing null or empty returns an empty list without querying the index'. Apply the same documentation update to executeSurfacePattern and executeDependencyPattern for consistency.
evidence: BlackLabQueryExecutor.findCollocations: 'if (lemma == null || lemma.isEmpty()) { logger.debug("skipping query — lemma is null or empty"); return Collections.emptyList(); }'
QueryExecutor.findCollocations Javadoc documents @param lemma and @throws but does not mention the null/empty silent-empty behavior — callers reading the interface cannot know this.
Similarly executeSurfacePattern and executeDependencyPattern have the same silent-empty guard without interface-level documentation.

issues.summary: BlackLabConllUIndexer.indexFile Javadoc references private method indexDirectory that callers cannot access, misleading the API consumer
issues.suggestion: Remove the 'prefer indexDirectory' sentence from indexFile Javadoc. If indexDirectory is intended to be public in future, add a TODO comment in the method body instead. If it is dead code, remove the method entirely (as noted in the high_level_elegance review).
evidence: indexFile Javadoc: 'prefer indexDirectory when the intent is explicitly to walk a directory with a glob pattern.'
indexDirectory is private — callers cannot call it. The Javadoc recommendation is therefore impossible to follow and misleading.

issues.summary: Multi-seed handler parses and validates 'nouns_per' before rejecting it as unsupported
issues.suggestion: Check for the 'nouns_per' key and reject it before calling validateExploreRequest, or add the rejection logic inside validateExploreRequest with a method parameter listing unsupported keys.
evidence: validateExploreRequest calls resolveExploreParams which calls HttpApiUtils.parseIntParam(params, 'nouns_per', 30) — throws IllegalArgumentException on malformed input

handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti then checks 'if (req.params().containsKey("nouns_per"))' and sends 400 'not supported' — but the check runs after parsing, so a malformed nouns_per=abc returns a NumberFormat error, not the clearer 'not supported' message

issues.summary: executeSurfacePattern injects null posMap when empty map would suffice
issues.suggestion: Pass lemmaPosMap directly without the null-conversion conditional; simplify to just 'lemmaPosMap' and let the callee handle empty maps.
evidence: return collocateQueryHelper.buildAndRankCollocates(freqMap, headwordFreq, minLogDice, maxResults, lemmaPosMap.isEmpty() ? null : lemmaPosMap);
buildAndRankCollocates treats null and empty map identically — both result in UNKNOWN_POS for all collocates

issues.summary: All API responses built as Map<String, Object> with no compile-time shape validation
issues.suggestion: Introduce typed response record classes (e.g. SketchResponse, ExploreResponse) and serialize them to JSON via a serialization utility. This captures response contracts in the type system and allows compile-time detection of field name/type regressions.
evidence: ExplorationHandlers.buildBaseExploreResponse: Map<String, Object> response = new HashMap<>(); response.put("status", "ok"); response.put("relation_type", relationType); — keys are literal strings, values are Object
SketchHandlers.formatWordSketchResult: Map<String, Object> word = new HashMap<>(); word.put("lemma", result.lemma()); — response shape defined only by these string puts
BcqlHandlers.buildBcqlResponse: response.put("total_results", results.size()); — an int put as Object, no type contract

issues.summary: executeAndFormatCollocations returns null as a sentinel for empty results instead of Optional
issues.suggestion: Change return type to Optional<ExecutedSketch> and return Optional.empty() for the no-results case. Update all call sites to use ifPresent or map.
evidence: private ExecutedSketch executeAndFormatCollocations(...) throws IOException { ... if (results.isEmpty()) return null; ... }
Callers: 'if (sketch != null) { byRelation.put(rel.id(), builder.apply(rel, sketch)); }' — null check required at call site
Return type is ExecutedSketch not Optional<ExecutedSketch>, so nullability is not expressed in the type

issues.summary: collectDependencyFrequencies understates its scope — the method also ranks and returns results
issues.suggestion: Rename to executeDependencyPattern or runDependencyPatternQuery to reflect that the method executes a query and returns ranked results, not merely collects frequencies.
evidence: private List<QueryResults.WordSketchResult> collectDependencyFrequencies(String bcql, String lemma, double minLogDice, int maxResults) — returns ranked WordSketchResult list
The method calls executeCollocateSearch, collectFrequenciesFromGroups, and buildAndRankCollocates — doing far more than collecting frequencies

issues.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser exposes four lemma-extraction methods whose names don't clearly communicate their differences
issues.suggestion: Rename to make the contract visible in the name: extractLastLemmaLowercased (for extractLemmaFromMatch), extractLastLemmaOrEmpty (for extractLemmaWithFallback), and drop extractCollocateLemma as a thin alias. Add a brief Javadoc table to the class summarising when to use each.
evidence: extractLemmaFromMatch(String matchText) — lowercases and returns @Nullable
extractLemmaWithFallback(String identity) — falls back to plain-text last-token, returns empty string
extractCollocateLemma(String matchXml) — delegates to extractLastLemma (original case)
extractLastLemma(String xml) — private, the actual regex implementation
extractLemmaFromMatch and extractCollocateLemma both call extractLastLemma but differ in return-type contract (nullable vs passthrough) and lowercasing

issues.summary: handleBcqlQueryPost has redundant HTTP-method suffix inconsistent with all sibling handler methods
issues.suggestion: Rename to handleBcqlQuery to match the convention of all sibling handler methods.
evidence: void handleBcqlQueryPost(HttpExchange exchange) — 'Post' suffix is not used by any other handle* method
Sibling methods: handleSketchRequest, handleSemanticFieldExplore, handleConcordanceExamples, handleVisualRadial — none carry HTTP method suffixes

issues.summary: requireParam and parseBcqlRequest use null-return as abort signal while sibling helpers throw IllegalArgumentException
issues.suggestion: Unify on the exception-based approach: have requireParam throw IllegalArgumentException (letting wrapWithErrorHandling handle the 400). This eliminates the null-check pattern at call sites and makes the error strategy consistent throughout the handler layer.
evidence: HttpApiUtils.requireParam: sends 400 and returns null; callers must null-check before proceeding
HttpApiUtils.parseIntParam: throws IllegalArgumentException on invalid input; wrapWithErrorHandling converts to 400
BcqlHandlers.parseBcqlRequest: sends error and returns null BcqlRequest; handleBcqlQueryPost checks 'if (req == null) return;'
These two patterns coexist in the same API layer with no documented reason for the split

issues.summary: executeRelationQueries catches RuntimeException alongside IOException, masking programming errors as partial-result warnings
issues.suggestion: Narrow the catch to IOException only. Let RuntimeExceptions propagate to wrapWithErrorHandling which will log them fully and return a 500, making programming errors visible.
evidence: catch (IOException | RuntimeException e) { logger.warn("Relation {} failed for lemma {}: {}", rel.id(), lemma, e.getMessage()); relationErrors.add(rel.id() + ": " + e.getMessage()); }
NullPointerException, ClassCastException, or any other programming error would be silently collected as a relation error string rather than propagating as a 500

issues.summary: BlackLabConllUIndexer.errorOccurred swallows indexing errors with no propagation path through close()
issues.suggestion: Expose getErrorCount() as a public method, or throw an IOException from close() when errorCount > 0 (with a flag to control this behavior). This gives callers a reliable way to detect partial index failures.
evidence: errorOccurred returns true (continue) and increments errorCount — errors are counted but not rethrown
close() logs errorCount > 0 as a warning but does not throw or return an error indicator
Callers of indexFile()/close() have no way to detect that indexing errors occurred other than reading the count via getDocumentCount() (but getErrorCount() is not a public method)

issues.summary: GrammarConfigLoader.createDefaultEnglish() reads system property each call — two calls with different -D settings yield different configs
issues.suggestion: If createDefaultEnglish is intended as a one-time startup factory, document this constraint with @ApiNote or a single-call assertion. Alternatively, cache the result in a static field with double-checked locking if repeated calls are anticipated.
evidence: public static GrammarConfig createDefaultEnglish() { String path = System.getProperty("grammar.config", "grammars/relations.json"); ... return load(configPath); }
The method is currently only called once during startup in Main.handleBlackLabServerCommand, so the risk is latent rather than active

issues.summary: BcqlHandlers uses a protocol acronym while sibling handlers use domain concept names
issues.suggestion: Rename to CorpusQueryHandlers or ArbitraryQueryHandlers to align with the domain-naming pattern of all sibling handler classes.
evidence: Sibling classes: SketchHandlers, ExplorationHandlers, ConcordanceHandlers, VisualizationHandlers — all named after the domain concern they serve
BcqlHandlers is named after the query language protocol (BCQL), not the domain concern (arbitrary corpus queries)

issues.summary: LogDiceCalculator uses *Calculator suffix while all other utility helpers use *Utils suffix
issues.suggestion: Rename to LogDiceUtils for consistency, or adopt *Calculator as the canonical suffix and rename CqlUtils and RelationUtils to match. Either choice removes the inconsistency.
evidence: CqlUtils, RelationUtils follow the *Utils pattern
LogDiceCalculator uses *Calculator — single deviation in the utils package

issues.summary: ExplorationResult is a plain class while most other model types are records, with no documented rationale
issues.suggestion: Add a comment in ExplorationResult explaining why it is a class rather than a record (e.g., 'plain class rather than record because it provides filtering utility methods'). Alternatively, evaluate whether it could become a record with utility as static methods on ExplorationResultUtils.
evidence: CoreCollocate, AdjectiveProfile, ComparisonResult, ExploreOptions, SingleSeedExploreOptions are all Java records

ExplorationResult is a plain class with private final fields and explicit getter methods — has utility methods (getTopNouns, getNounsWithMinShared, isEmpty) which justify the class, but this rationale is not documented

issues.summary: Individual HTTP handler classes lack dedicated unit tests — dispatch logic and parameter handling paths are not explicitly covered
issues.suggestion: Add dedicated unit tests for each handler class using mock QueryExecutor and GrammarConfig. At minimum test: SketchHandlers dispatch (dep/no-dep/relation/full), ExplorationHandlers multi-seed param rejection, BcqlHandlers JSON parse error path.
evidence: No test files named SketchHandlersTest, ExplorationHandlersTest, BcqlHandlersTest, or ConcordanceHandlersTest found
HandlersTest.java exists but its scope is unclear — may not cover all dispatch paths in SketchHandlers.handleSketchRequest (6 distinct code paths: dep/no-dep, specific-relation/full-sketch)
ExplorationHandlers.handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti has a specific logic ordering issue (nouns_per parsed before rejection) that would be caught by a targeted test

issues.summary: Main.java CLI argument parsing logic (flag loops, requireNextArg, dispatch) is not tested
issues.suggestion: Extract argument parsing into a dedicated CliArgs class or parsing method that returns a parsed options record and throws IllegalArgumentException instead of calling System.exit. This makes the parsing testable without side effects.
evidence: No MainTest.java or CliParsingTest.java found in test tree
handleBlackLabIndexCommand, handleBlackLabQueryCommand parse flag loops with switch statements — missing --input or --lemma silently returns after printing an error
requireNextArg calls System.exit(1) — this behavior cannot be tested without a security manager or test harness

issues.summary: requireParam and parseIntParam/parseDoubleParam have different signatures and error strategies for request parameter validation
issues.suggestion: Unify on the exception-based approach: rewrite requireParam to throw IllegalArgumentException('Missing required parameter: name') and remove the HttpExchange parameter. wrapWithErrorHandling already catches IllegalArgumentException and returns 400. This makes all parameter validation consistent and removes the null-check pattern.
evidence: requireParam(HttpExchange exchange, Map<String,String> params, String name) — takes exchange, returns null and sends 400 on missing
parseIntParam(Map<String,String> params, String name, int defaultValue) — no exchange, throws IllegalArgumentException on invalid value
Callers must know which pattern applies: required params use requireParam+null-check, optional numeric params use parseIntParam+try-catch-in-wrapper

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer exposes a 5-parameter package-private overload that leaks raw pattern strings above the config abstraction
issues.suggestion: Make the 5-param overload private and create a dedicated test-helper that constructs a RelationConfig with the desired patterns instead of calling the internal overload directly from tests.
evidence: package-private ExplorationResult exploreByPattern(String seed, String relationName, String bcqlPattern, String simplePattern, SingleSeedExploreOptions opts)
The public API takes RelationConfig and SingleSeedExploreOptions — callers don't need raw pattern strings
The internal overload is package-private and called by tests directly, creating a coupling between tests and internal implementation details

issues.summary: Entire access control relies on loopback binding with no auth fallback if network accessibility is ever added
issues.suggestion: If this tool ever needs to be accessible beyond localhost (e.g., on a local network or as a shared research service), add an API key header check as a minimum access control layer. Document the loopback-only deployment constraint explicitly in README to signal that this is not safe to expose on a broader network without adding auth.
evidence: this.server = com.sun.net.httpserver.HttpServer.create(new InetSocketAddress(InetAddress.getLoopbackAddress(), port), 0);
Changing InetAddress.getLoopbackAddress() to new InetSocketAddress(port) would expose all endpoints with no auth mechanism in place
POST /api/bcql would allow arbitrary corpus queries against the index from any client

issues.summary: deriveNounCqlConstraint and deriveAdjectivePattern duplicate a complex double-fallback stream pattern that should be extracted
issues.suggestion: Extract a shared GrammarConfig utility method: RelationConfig.findBestRelationForPosGroup(GrammarConfig, PosGroup, RelationType...) that encapsulates the priority-ordered search. Replace both deriveNounCqlConstraint and deriveAdjectivePattern with calls to this utility.
evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer.deriveNounCqlConstraint: .findFirst().map(...).orElseGet(() -> grammarConfig.getRelations().stream().filter(...).findFirst().map(...).orElse(FALLBACK))
CollocateProfileComparator.deriveAdjectivePattern: structurally identical double-fallback stream with different type filters
Both methods have the same structure: (1) filter by specific relation type AND POS group, (2) fall back to any matching POS group, (3) fall back to hardcoded constant

issues.summary: SketchHandlers uses position-based array indexing for URL routing — fragile to path structure changes
issues.suggestion: Replace array-position routing with named segment extraction (a simple utility method that maps URL pattern /api/sketch/{lemma}/{sub1}/{sub2} to named fields). Even a simple record SketchPath(String lemma, String sub1, String sub2) would make the intent explicit and changes local.
evidence: String[] parts = path.substring("/api/sketch/".length()).split("/");
if (parts.length > 1 && "dep".equals(parts[1])) — position 1 is 'dep' keyword
String specificDeprel = parts.length > 2 ? parts[2] : null; — position 2 is the deprel
Adding /api/sketch/{lemma}/surface/{relation} would require updating position checks throughout

issues.summary: ConlluConverter.convertConlluToWplChunks mixes CoNLL-U sentence parsing state machine with chunk file rotation logic
issues.suggestion: Extract sentence-boundary parsing into a private method processLine(line, state) that emits tokens/events, and keep chunk rotation logic in the main loop. This separation would make the finally-block counting logic easier to verify and make both concerns independently testable.
evidence: Single method manages: inSentence boolean (sentence boundary tracking), sentencesInChunk counter (rotation trigger), writer management (open/close/rotate), and the complex finally block for counting
The method is ~60 lines with a 3-level nested try-finally and two distinct state machines interleaved
